maml:description.maml:para: Adds Azure subscription data to lab

The cmdlet adds the Azure subscription data from an Azure RM profile generated by Save-AzureRmContext to the lab definition, creates or selects the default resource group, default location and default storage account. It caches the possible VM sizes and installation offers/skus for the selected location. The location can either be pre-selected or auto-generated based on response time.
The default location to choose
OBSOLETE PARAMETER WHICH WILL BE IGNORED The default storage account to choose
The default resource group to choose
Indicates that a Bastion host will be deployed
Time that machines will be shut down
Time zone for shutdown time. Defaults to current system timezone
Indicates that the endpoint is running Azure Stack Hub with its considerably older API versions.
Azure environment to use. Defaults to AzureGlobal. Intended use: Adding a connected Azure Stack environment. Theoretically possible but untested: Other environments like AzureChina.
Returns the lab's subscription
Subscription GUID
The name of the subscription to select, if necessary

maml:description.maml:para: Adds user rights on lab machines

Adds user rights on a lab machine from the following rights: 'SeNetworkLogonRight', 'SeRemoteInteractiveLogonRight', 'SeBatchLogonRight', 'SeInteractiveLogonRight', 'SeServiceLogonRight', 'SeDenyNetworkLogonRight', 'SeDenyInteractiveLogonRight', 'SeDenyBatchLogonRight', 'SeDenyServiceLogonRight', 'SeDenyRemoteInteractiveLogonRight', 'SeTcbPrivilege', 'SeMachineAccountPrivilege', 'SeIncreaseQuotaPrivilege', 'SeBackupPrivilege', 'SeChangeNotifyPrivilege', 'SeSystemTimePrivilege', 'SeCreateTokenPrivilege', 'SeCreatePagefilePrivilege', 'SeCreateGlobalPrivilege', 'SeDebugPrivilege', 'SeEnableDelegationPrivilege', 'SeRemoteShutdownPrivilege', 'SeAuditPrivilege', 'SeImpersonatePrivilege', 'SeIncreaseBasePriorityPrivilege', 'SeLoadDriverPrivilege', 'SeLockMemoryPrivilege', 'SeSecurityPrivilege', 'SeSystemEnvironmentPrivilege', 'SeManageVolumePrivilege', 'SeProfileSingleProcessPrivilege', 'SeSystemProfilePrivilege', 'SeUndockPrivilege', 'SeAssignPrimaryTokenPrivilege', 'SeRestorePrivilege', 'SeShutdownPrivilege', 'SeSynchAgentPrivilege', 'SeTakeOwnershipPrivilege'
The computer name the rights are being granted on
A list of privileges to assign, e.g. SEDebug
The user name to be granted rights
The computer name the rights are being granted on
A list of privileges to assign, e.g. SEDebug
The user name to be granted rights

Clears the variable $script:data to effectively clear the lab definition

dev:remarks.maml:para: Clear current lab, so that Get-Lab and Get-LabDefinition will not return

maml:description.maml:para: Clear the lab cache
Removes the automated lab cache from the Windows registry: HKEY_CURRENT_USER\Software\AutomatedLab\Cache

dev:remarks.maml:para: Clears all of AutomatedLab's caches, meaning that all timestamps and the cached ISOs will be removed. During the next lab installation, the caches will be updated again. Useful when running into issues with the available operating systems.

maml:description.maml:para: Connect two labs via VPN

This cmdlet allows you to connect two labs through a persistent IPSEC VPN connection. Currently connections from Hyper-V labs to Azure labs, connections from Hyper-V labs to any VPN Gateway and connections of two Azure labs are possible.
To make use of this cmdlet, your on-premises lab needs to contain a router, i.e. a machine with the Routing role and one external NIC.
The source lab name
S2S VPN: The IP address of the remote VPN gateway
The pre-shared key for the S2S VPN
One or more address spaces to be routed in case you are connecting to an external VPN gateway and not to another lab. Example: 192.168.2.30/24
Optionally specify the adapter to use
The source lab name
The destination lab name
Optionally specify the adapter to use
One or more address spaces to be routed in case you are connecting to an external VPN gateway and not to another lab. Example: 192.168.2.30/24
S2S VPN: The IP address of the remote VPN gateway
The destination lab name
Optionally specify the adapter to use
The pre-shared key for the S2S VPN
The source lab name

dev:remarks.maml:para: This connects the two existing labs, MyOnPremisesLab - a Hyper-V lab with at least one router - and MyAzureLab, which is any Azure based lab.
On Azure, the resources Virtual Network Gateway, Local Network Gateway, Public IP Address and Virtual Network Connection will be created. On Hyper-V, S2SVPN will be enabled on the router.

This command connects the Hyper-V lab MyOnPremLab to a VPN gateway listening on myvpngateway.mycompany.com. Requests to one of the address spaces "192.168.30.0/24", "192.168.60.0/24" and "192.168.90.0/24" will be routed through the VPN connection

maml:description.maml:para: Configures several local policy settings to enable lab host remoting

Configures several local policy settings to enable lab host remoting. These are: SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\CredentialsDelegation\AllowFreshCredentials, set to 1 SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\CredentialsDelegation\AllowFreshCredentialsWhenNTLMOnly, set to 1 SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\CredentialsDelegation\AllowSavedCredentials, set to 1 SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\CredentialsDelegation\AllowSavedCredentialsWhenNTLMOnly, set to 1 SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Policies\System\CredSSP\Parameters -Name AllowEncryptionOracle, set to 2
Do not query user.

maml:description.maml:para: Enable the transmission of telemetry
Enable the transmission of telemetry by setting AUTOMATEDLAB_TELEMETRY_OPTOUT to 0
The full report can be accessed at <https://app.powerbi.com/view?r=eyJrIjoiMmYyYTdmODUtMDJlZS00M2QwLWE1MDgtMGU5YTkyODVhZmQ2IiwidCI6Ijc5MzlmZDI1LTQ0YjktNGNjMC04YjVkLWRmZGZjYTg2ZTZlYyIsImMiOjl9>
The following information will be collected - Your country (IP addresses are by default set to 0.0.0.0 by Azure Application Insights after the location is extracted)
- Your number of lab machines
- The roles you used (including custom roles, so be careful with your naming pattern)
- The time it took your lab to finish
- The lifetime of your lab (collected once Remove-Lab is executed)
- Your AutomatedLab version, the PS Version, OS Version and the lab's Hypervisor type

dev:remarks.maml:para: Enables telemetry

maml:description.maml:para: Get AutomatedLab settings
Get-LabConfigurationItem is a wrapper to retrieve configuration settings through PSFramework (before that: Datum)

A default value that should be returned in case there is no such setting

The name of the setting, e.g. Timeout_RestartLabMachine_Shutdown

maml:description.maml:para: Starts the process of lab deployment

If called without any parameters, Install-Lab will: - Create all necessary base (parent) disks for all operating systems to be used in the lab - Create all virtual networks to be used in the lab - Create all machines to be used in the lab - Deploy and configure all machines defined in the lab in following order: Root DCs Routing DHCP Servers First child DCs Additional DCs Create AD trusts Certificate (CA) servers SQL servers 2008/2008R2/2012/2014 Web servers Orchestrator servers 2012 Exchange servers 2013 Sharepoint servers 2013 Visual Studio Office 2013 Start any machines without any AutomatedLab roles
If called with parameters, only the action(s) specified by the parameters will be performed.
Seconds to wait between starting each lab machine
Install all ADFS components
Start configuring of AD trusts between all AD forests in the lab
Deploy only Azure services
Create base images (parent disks)
Start installation of all Certificate (CA) servers
Deploy ConfigMgr
Create checkpoints between each AutomatedLab role. Ie Checkpoint between AD and CA and SQL etc
Start installation of DHCP servers
Start installation of Root DCs, First Child DCs and additional DCs. Note that lab machines with Routing role (if any), will be installed between Root DCs and First Child DCs.
Install all DSC Pull Servers
Deploy Dynamics 365
Deploy only Failover Clusters
Deploy ISCSI targets
Deploy File Servers
Deploy Hyper-V VMs
Install certificates of virtual machines
Add lab VMs to SSH known hosts file
Create virtual networks
Disable validation of the lab configuration (and thereby missing out on avoiding common configuration errors)
Start installation of Office 2013 on all lab machines with this defined
Install all Office 2016 Servers
Start installation of all Orchestrator 2012 servers
Run post-deployment tests using Pester
Start all post insallations of machines with this defined
Deploy Remote Desktop Services
Start installation of machines with Routing role
Deploy SCOM
Deploy SCVMM
Start installation of all Sharepoint servers
Start installation of all SQL servers. SQL servers will be installed in batches of 4.
Start all remaining machines which are not already started
Deploy only CI/CD servers
Start installation of Visual Studio on all lab machines with this defined
Create lab machines
WebServers
Deploy Windows Admin Center
Install all ADFS components
Start configuring of AD trusts between all AD forests in the lab
Deploy only Azure services
Create base images (parent disks)
Start installation of all Certificate (CA) servers
Deploy ConfigMgr
Create checkpoints between each AutomatedLab role. Ie Checkpoint between AD and CA and SQL etc
Seconds to wait between starting each lab machine
Start installation of DHCP servers
Start installation of Root DCs, First Child DCs and additional DCs. Note that lab machines with Routing role (if any), will be installed between Root DCs and First Child DCs.
Install all DSC Pull Servers
Deploy Dynamics 365
Deploy only Failover Clusters
Deploy ISCSI targets
Deploy File Servers
Deploy Hyper-V VMs
Install certificates of virtual machines
Add lab VMs to SSH known hosts file
Create virtual networks
Disable validation of the lab configuration (and thereby missing out on avoiding common configuration errors)
Start installation of Office 2013 on all lab machines with this defined
Install all Office 2016 Servers
Start installation of all Orchestrator 2012 servers
Run post-deployment tests using Pester
Start all post insallations of machines with this defined
Deploy Remote Desktop Services
Start installation of machines with Routing role
Deploy SCOM
Deploy SCVMM
Start installation of all Sharepoint servers
Start installation of all SQL servers. SQL servers will be installed in batches of 4.
Start all remaining machines which are not already started
Deploy only CI/CD servers
Start installation of Visual Studio on all lab machines with this defined
Create lab machines
WebServers
Deploy Windows Admin Center

dev:remarks.maml:para: Deploy and configure all machines defined in the lab in following order: Root DCs Routing DHCP Servers First child DCs Additional DCs Create AD trusts Certificate (CA) servers SQL servers 2008/2008R2/2012/2014 Web servers Orchestrator servers 2012 Exchange servers 2013 Sharepoint servers Visual Studio Office 2013 Start any machines without any AutomatedLab roles
Creates all needed base images if these are not already present. Size and/or integrity is NOT checked/verified. If the base image (disk) exists, it will considered good.

Creates all needed base images if these are not already present. Size and/or integrity is NOT checked/verified. If the base image (disk) exists, it will considered good. All virtual network to be used in lab will be created.
Same as first example but lab will be attempted validated/checked for common configuration errors. Not recommended. Use only if experiencing issues.

Deployment/configuration of all domain controllers, all Certificate (CA) servers, SQL servers and Web servers will be performed. There will be a deplay of 30 seconds start of each machine if more than one machine is required to start at the same time. Use to avoid disk congestion (spread the 'load').
Remaining machines (typically those without any AutomatedLab role), will be started and checked for readiness before contiuing.
None

maml:description.maml:para: Create forest trust
Creates DNS forwarders and a bidirectional trust for all forests in the current lab

dev:remarks.maml:para: Creates DNS forwarders and a bidirectional trust for all forests in the current lab

maml:description.maml:para: Enable ADFS

Configures the Active Directory Federation Services for the current lab by generating certificates from the lab CA, creating a service user and the necessary DNS entries as well as setting up an ADFS farm

dev:remarks.maml:para: Configures the Active Directory Federation Services for the current lab by generating certificates from the lab CA, creating a service user and the necessary DNS entries as well as setting up an ADFS farm

maml:description.maml:para: Create ADFS proxy
Configures an ADFS proxy for existing ADFS lab machines by retrieving a valid certificate for one ADFS machine and then installing a web application proxy

maml:description.maml:para: Create and populate a new labsources folder

Create and populate a new labsources folder. The labsources folder contains a bunch of useful or necessary components. When the cmdlet is executed for the first time, the lab sources content will be pulled from the master branch of the GitHub repository AutomatedLab.
This cmdlet can be used to update sample scripts. Be aware that it will overwrite your changes to any files.
The local lab sources can optionally be synced with Azure, all Lab cmdlets are aware of that and select the correct location for you, if you use the variable $LabSources

Folder content: DscConfigurations - Lab DSC configurations that have been applied to machines GitRepositories - Cloned repositories that have been pushed to your lab CI/CD release pipeline ISOs - Essential. Should contain OS ISOs you deploy your VMs from, can optionally contain product ISOs like SQL OSUpdates - Intended for OS updates, not automatically used PostInstallationActivities - Used in many samples, this folder contains some tasks like setting up a DSC Pull Server or creating thousands of users. Sample Scripts - Contains a wide range of sample scripts for you to play with SoftwarePackages - Intended for installers and the like Tools - Intended for little tools that e.g. get copied to each machine, if enabled
The drive to store LabSources on
Name of branch to use when downloading content. Defaults to master
Only scaffold empty folder structure
Indicates that content will be replaced
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.
Name of branch to use when downloading content. Defaults to master
The drive to store LabSources on
Only scaffold empty folder structure
Indicates that content will be replaced
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.
